edges.description: SMCHD1 variant effects alter neural crest transcriptional and signaling programs relevant to craniofacial morphogenesis.
edges.target: Neural crest migration and AKT signaling imbalance

edges.description: Neural crest migration and signaling imbalance disrupts nasal and olfactory structural development.
edges.source: Neural crest migration and AKT signaling imbalance

edges.description: Nasal and olfactory developmental disruption is accompanied by ocular, midface, dental, and reproductive anomalies.
edges.target: Craniofacial, ocular, and reproductive malformation syndrome

evidence.reference_title: Hyposmia-nasal and ocular hypoplasia-hypogonadotropic hypogonadism syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0000309 | Abnormal midface morphology | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: SMCHD1-linked developmental disruption impairs formation of the external nose, nares, paranasal sinuses, and olfactory bulbs or placode-related structures.

evidence.reference_title: De novo mutations in SMCHD1 cause Bosma arhinia microphthalmia syndrome and abrogate nasal development.
evidence.snippet: SMCHD1 as a key player in nasal development

evidence.reference_title: SMCHD1 mutations associated with a rare muscular dystrophy can also cause isolated arhinia and Bosma arhinia microphthalmia syndrome.
evidence.snippet: CRISPR/Cas9-mediated alteration of smchd1 in zebrafish yielded arhinia-relevant phenotypes.

evidence.reference_title: Bosma Arhinia Microphthalmia Syndrome (BAMS): First Report from Vietnam.
evidence.snippet: absence of paranasal sinuses and olfactory bulbs.

evidence.reference_title: Hyposmia-nasal and ocular hypoplasia-hypogonadotropic hypogonadism syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0009927 | Aplasia of the nose | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: The downstream clinical syndrome combines arhinia or severe nasal hypoplasia, ocular hypoplasia or microphthalmia, hyposmia or anosmia, dental and palatal anomalies, inguinal hernia, and hypogonadotropic hypogonadism with generally normal intelligence.
nodes.id: Craniofacial, ocular, and reproductive malformation syndrome

evidence.reference_title: Hypoplasia of the nose and eyes, hyposmia, hypogeusia, and hypogonadotrophic hypogonadism in two males.
evidence.snippet: Two males, 9-11 and 29-31 years of age, with severe hypoplasia of the nose, hypoplasia of the eyes, sensory abnormalities of taste and smell, and hypogonadism were studied.
severe hypoplasia of the nose and eyes, palatal abnormalities, deficient taste and smell, inguinal hernias, hypogonadotropic hypogonadism with cryptorchidism, and normal intelligence.

nodes.description: Patient-derived neural crest cells show altered PI3K/AKT-related signaling, extracellular matrix and cell-adhesion programs, and reduced migration, providing a cellular mechanism for craniofacial malformations.
nodes.id: Neural crest migration and AKT signaling imbalance

evidence.reference_title: AKT Signaling Modifies the Balance between Cell Proliferation and Migration in Neural Crest Cells from Patients Affected with Bosma Arhinia and Microphthalmia Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: identify pathways associated with the BAMS phenotype and related neural crest defects

evidence.reference_title: AKT Signaling Modifies the Balance between Cell Proliferation and Migration in Neural Crest Cells from Patients Affected with Bosma Arhinia and Microphthalmia Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: defects in neural crest migration might contribute to the craniofacial anomalies in BAMS.

evidence.reference_title: AKT Signaling Modifies the Balance between Cell Proliferation and Migration in Neural Crest Cells from Patients Affected with Bosma Arhinia and Microphthalmia Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: BAMS NCSCs are characterized by an overall decrease in the expression of a number genes required for NCSC migration

nodes.description: Heterozygous SMCHD1 missense variants in the extended ATPase domain alter the epigenetic regulator's activity, producing disease-specific chromatin and methylation effects distinct from the broader SMCHD1 variant spectrum seen in FSHD2.

evidence.reference_title: De novo mutations in SMCHD1 cause Bosma arhinia microphthalmia syndrome and abrogate nasal development.
evidence.snippet: in vivo assays in Xenopus laevis embryos suggest that these mutations may behave as gain-of-function alleles.

evidence.reference_title: SMCHD1 mutation spectrum for facioscapulohumeral muscular dystrophy type 2 (FSHD2) and Bosma arhinia microphthalmia syndrome (BAMS) reveals disease-specific localisation of variants in the ATPase domain.
evidence.snippet: BAMS-associated SMCHD1 variants result in quantifiable local DNA hypomethylation.

meta.association: Heterozygous missense variants, usually de novo

evidence.reference_title: Hyposmia-nasal and ocular hypoplasia-hypogonadotropic hypogonadism syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: SMCHD1 | structural maintenance of chromosomes flexible hinge domain containing 1 | hgnc:29090 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) in

evidence.reference_title: De novo mutations in SMCHD1 cause Bosma arhinia microphthalmia syndrome and abrogate nasal development.
evidence.snippet: missense mutations in the epigenetic regulator SMCHD1 mapping to the extended ATPase domain of the encoded protein cause BAMS in all 14 cases studied.

evidence.reference_title: SMCHD1 mutations associated with a rare muscular dystrophy can also cause isolated arhinia and Bosma arhinia microphthalmia syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Sequencing of 40 people with arhinia revealed that 84% of probands harbor a missense mutation localized to a constrained region of SMCHD1 encompassing the ATPase domain.

evidence.reference_title: SMCHD1 mutation spectrum for facioscapulohumeral muscular dystrophy type 2 (FSHD2) and Bosma arhinia microphthalmia syndrome (BAMS) reveals disease-specific localisation of variants in the ATPase domain.
evidence.snippet: in BAMS, pathogenic variants are restricted to the extended ATPase domain.

evidence.reference_title: SMCHD1 / arhinia, choanal atresia, and microphthalmia (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: SMCHD1 | HGNC:29090 | arhinia, choanal atresia, and microphthalmia | MONDO:0011323 | AD | Definitive

nodes.description: Selected patients may undergo staged midface advancement, tissue expansion, and nasal construction to address absent nasal and midface structures.

evidence.reference_title: Nasal Construction in Congenital Arhinia Due to Novel SMCHD1 Gene Variant.
evidence.snippet: A staged surgical approach was applied.

evidence.reference_title: Nasal Construction in Congenital Arhinia Due to Novel SMCHD1 Gene Variant.
evidence.snippet: nasal construction with a forehead flap that was placed over a costochondral framework derived from rib cartilage.
